title: Dortmanna orizabae
intro: Dortmanna orizabae, also known as Mexican Dodder, is a plant species in the family Convolvulaceae. It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help alleviate conditions like arthritis and reduce oxidative stress in the body. The bioactive constituents of Dortmanna orizabae include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. The plant is commonly used in traditional medicine and is often prepared as a tea or tincture, although its exact preparation and dosage can vary depending on the intended use and cultural tradition.
taxonomy: Dortmanna orizabae is a species in the family Lythraceae. It belongs to the genus Dortmanna within the family Lythraceae. The classification of Dortmanna orizabae places it in the order Myrtales. At the kingdom level, it falls under Plantae, and at the phylum level, it falls under Angiosperms. This classification further categorizes Dortmanna orizabae into the clade flowering plants.
